title: Exceptions, Diagnostics, Exit Status, and Recovery · Code Recall
summary: A delayed maintenance review reconstructs exceptions, diagnostics, exit status, and recovery from repository state, input bytes, decoded text, tokens, counters, ranking keys, report records, stdout, stderr, exit status, tests, package artifact, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for causal error and recovery contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate expected path, admission, decoding, policy, and output failures into narrow domain errors without erasing causes.
Write concise actionable diagnostics containing operation and safe context while retaining a debuggable causal chain.
Map success, usage error, input error, analysis error, and output error to documented stable statuses.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

Build deterministic Python evidence to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance; preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Recovery Guidance, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Recovery Guidance precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Recovery Guidance, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A review Bookbot team handles case 7829 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to map expected failures to causal diagnostics, stable nonzero status, atomic output, and recovery guidance; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Give a bounded next action, preserve original inputs, and distinguish retryable from policy or data failures.

For Bookbot and Python Record what required effort · Review Recovery Guidance, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Retrying every file failure is a safe recovery plan.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Domain Errors to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Domain Errors.

checks.description: The decision applies Causal Diagnostics to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Causal Diagnostics.

checks.description: The decision applies Exit Status Map to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Exit Status Map.

checks.description: The decision applies Atomic Output to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Atomic Output.

checks.description: The decision applies Recovery Guidance to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Recovery Guidance.

checks.description: The decision applies Recovery Guidance to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Recovery Guidance.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using give and bounded plus specific case evidence.
